title: Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Abuse Resistance · Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed review reconstructs authorization, tenant isolation, and abuse resistance from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose role, attribute, relationship, capability, or policy models from resource, action, subject, context, and review needs.
Enforce authorization at every trusted resource and transition boundary using current server-side state.
Propagate tenant identity through query, cache, file, job, event, log, backup, restore, and support paths with adversarial tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Authorization Model
steps.instruction:
Order the Authorization Model evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Authorization Model.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Authorization Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Authorization Model, keep Authorization Model connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose role, attribute, relationship, capability, or policy models from resource, action, subject, context, and review needs.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Authorization Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

stimulus.title: Privileged Operation case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Administrators should have unrestricted standing access.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work. Artifact under review: deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Privileged Operation, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Privileged Operation
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Privileged Operation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. For Record what required effort · Review Privileged Operation, keep Privileged Operation connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Authorization Model to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Authorization Model.
